step_by_step_thinking:
To determine how to move the tongue away from the posterior wall of the pharynx effectively, we look toward the description of various airway opening procedures mentioned in the references. Techniques like the head-tilt/chin-lift and jaw-thrust are specifically discussed in relation to moving structures within the pharynx out of the way. Among them, the **jaw-thrust maneuver** directly addresses the issue by lifting the jaws without extending the neck excessively, making sure to avoid any unnecessary strain on the cervical spine especially important in cases of suspected cervical spine injury. Thus, option A is well-supported clinically. Hence,
